A, title V, § 549E135 Stat. 1726Dec. 27, 2021“Not later than 30 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of Defense shall establish or reconstitute, maintain, and ensure the full functionality of the Military Justice Review Panel established pursuant to , United States Code (article 146 of the Uniform Code of Military Justice)) [sic].”Dec. 27, 2021section 946 of title 10

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall establish a panel to conduct independent periodic reviews and assessments of the operation of this chapter. The panel shall be known as the “Military Justice Review Panel” (in this section referred to as the “Panel”).

children.heading: .—Number of members
children.label: 1 .—Number of members
children.text: The Panel shall be composed of thirteen members.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary of Defense (in consultation with the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating when it is not operating as a service in the Navy).

children.label: B
children.text: The Attorney General.

children.label: C
children.text: The Judge Advocates General of the Army, Navy, Air Force, and Coast Guard, and the Staff Judge Advocate to the Commandant of the Marine Corps.

children.heading: .—Appointment of remaining members by secretary of defense
children.label: 3 .—Appointment of remaining members by secretary of defense
A
children.text: The chairman and ranking minority member of the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives.

children.label: B
children.text: The Chief Justice of the United States.

children.label: C
children.text: The Chief Judge of the United States Court of Appeals for the Armed Forces.

children.label: A
children.text: Three members designated by the Secretary of Defense shall serve a term of two years.

children.label: B
children.text: Three members designated by the Secretary of Defense shall serve a term of four years.

children.label: C
children.text: Three members designated by the Secretary of Defense shall serve a term of six years.

children.label: D
children.text: Four members designated by the Secretary of Defense shall serve a term of eight years.

children.heading: .—Qualifications of Members
children.label: c .—Qualifications of Members
children.text: The members of the Panel shall be appointed from among private United States citizens with expertise in criminal law, as well as appropriate and diverse experience in investigation, prosecution, defense, victim representation, or adjudication with respect to courts-martial, Federal civilian courts, or State courts.

children.heading: .—Chair
children.label: d .—Chair
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall select the chair of the Panel from among the members.

children.heading: .—Term
children.label: 1 .—Term
children.text: Subject to subsection (b)(4) and paragraphs (2) and (3) of this subsection, each member shall be appointed for a term of eight years, and no member may serve more than one term.

children.heading: .—Vacancy
children.label: 2 .—Vacancy
children.text: Any vacancy in the Panel shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment. A member appointed to fill a vacancy in the Panel that occurs before the expiration of the term of appointment of the predecessor of such member shall be appointed for the remainder of the term of such predecessor.

children.label: A
children.text: the appointment of the member is to fill a vacancy described in subsection (b)(4); or

children.label: i
children.text: to a term of four years or less in accordance with subsection (b)(4); or

children.label: ii
children.text: to fill a vacancy that occurs before the expiration of the term of the predecessor of such member and for which the remainder of the term of such predecessor is four years or less.

children.heading: .—Initial review of recent amendments to ucmj
children.label: 1 .—Initial review of recent amendments to ucmj
children.text: During fiscal year 2021, the Panel shall conduct an initial review and assessment of the implementation of the amendments made to this chapter during the preceding five years. In conducting the initial review and assessment, the Panel may review such other aspects of the operation of this chapter as the Panel considers appropriate.

children.heading: .—Sentencing data collection and report
children.label: 2 .—Sentencing data collection and report
children.text: During fiscal year 2020, the Panel shall gather and analyze sentencing data collected from each of the armed forces from general and special courts-martial applying offense-based sentencing under (article 56). The sentencing data shall include the number of accused who request member sentencing and the number who request sentencing by military judge alone, the offenses which the accused were convicted of, and the resulting sentence for each offense in each case. The Judge Advocates General and the Staff Judge Advocate to the Commandant of the Marine Corps shall provide the sentencing data in the format and for the duration established by the chair of the Panel. The analysis under this paragraph shall be included in the assessment required by paragraph (1).section 856 of this title

children.heading: .—Periodic comprehensive reviews
children.label: 3 .—Periodic comprehensive reviews
children.text: During fiscal year 2024 and every eight years thereafter, the Panel shall conduct a comprehensive review and assessment of the operation of this chapter.

children.heading: .—Periodic interim reviews
children.label: 4 .—Periodic interim reviews
children.text: During fiscal year 2028 and every eight years thereafter, the Panel shall conduct an interim review and assessment of such other aspects of the operation of this chapter as the Panel considers appropriate. In addition, at the request of the Secretary of Defense, the Panel may, at any time, review and assess other specific matters relating to the operation of this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: shall set forth the results of the review and assessment concerned, including the findings and recommendations of the Panel; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be submitted not later than December 31 of the calendar year in which the review and assessment is concluded.

children.heading: .—Hearings
children.label: g .—Hearings
children.text: The Panel may hold such hearings, sit and act at such times and places, take such testimony, and receive such evidence as the Panel considers appropriate to carry out its duties under this section.

children.heading: .—Information From Federal Agencies
children.label: h .—Information From Federal Agencies
children.text: Upon request of the chair of the Panel, a department or agency of the Federal Government shall provide information that the Panel considers necessary to carry out its duties under this section.

children.heading: .—Members to serve without pay
children.label: 1 .—Members to serve without pay
children.text: Members of the Panel shall serve without pay, but shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Panel.

children.heading: .—Staffing and resources
children.label: 2 .—Staffing and resources
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall provide staffing and resources to support the Panel.

children.heading: 105.—Chapterof Title
children.label: j 105.—Chapterof Title
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to the Panel.
